'''Joshua Drinkwater''' (born 15 June 1992) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He has also played for the [[London Broncos]], [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. Drinkwater won the [[2018 Challenge Cup]] with [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]].

==Background==
Drinkwater was born in [[Sydney|Sydney, New South Wales]], Australia, and is of [[Germans|German]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/05/02/garzaniti-eager-to-fill-drinkwaters-void/ |title=Garzaniti eager to fill Drinkwater's void |publisher=NSWRL |date=2 May 2018}}</ref> He is the [[brother]] of fellow [[rugby league]] footballer, [[Scott Drinkwater]] who plays for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

Drinkwater played his junior football for the [[Terrigal Sharks]], where he partnered best friend Josh English in the halves, before being signed by the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Playing career==
===National Youth Competition===
From 2010 to 2012, Drinkwater played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]' [[NRL Under-20s|National Youth Competition]] (NYC) team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/d/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304003647/http://nycdatabase.org/players/d/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=4 March 2016|title=D|publisher=Nyc Database|date=27 December 2012|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Josh-Drinkwater_3094/Matches/|title=Josh Drinkwater player results, scores, news and statistics|publisher=Rleague.com|accessdate=2013-05-16|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130930003519/http://rleague.com/players/Josh-Drinkwater_3094/Matches/|archivedate=30 September 2013}}</ref>

He was [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]]'s [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] top point-scorer in [[2012 NRL Under-20s season|2012]], with 137 points from 10 tries, 48 goals and 1 field goal.

On 4 September 2012, he signed a two-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] starting in 2013, where he was seen as a '''“'''''possible saviour to a club reeling in the post-[[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] era.'''''”'''<ref name="nrl">{{cite web| publisher= National Rugby League|title=Tigers halfback seeks NRL redemption| first = Jack| last = Brady| url=http://www.nrl.com/tigers-halfback-seeks-nrl-redemption/tabid/10874/newsid/83808/default.aspx|accessdate=4 March 2015|date=12 February 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/63319|title=Dragons Sign Promising Talents|publisher=Dragons.com.au|date=2012-09-04|accessdate=2013-05-16|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121127153613/http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/63319|archivedate=27 November 2012}}</ref>

===St. George Illawarra Dragons (2013)===
[[File:JOSH DRINKWATER.jpg|thumb|upright|Drinkwater playing for the [[Illawarra Cutters]] in 2013]]
In round 5 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Drinkwater made his [[National Rugby League]] début for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] against the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2013/round-5/st-george-illawarra-vs-newcastle/summary.html|title=NRL 2013 – Round 5|publisher=Rugby League Project|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

He finished off his début year in the [[NRL]] having only played in four games.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/OS0xNjI5LTQtLS0tLS0yMDIxOS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLQ==|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-02-12|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
In February 2014, Drinkwater joined the [[London Broncos]] effective immediately for the rest of the season in a straight swap for [[Michael Witt]], after being released from the final year of his [[St. George Illawarra]] contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=Michael Carayannis|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/st-george-illawarra-dragons-pair-snapped-up-by-london-broncos-20140222-338ng.html|title=St George Illawarra Dragons pair snapped up by London Broncos|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2014-02-22|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

He scored 136 points for the [[London Broncos]], but the club managed just one victory for the entire [[Super League]] season.<ref name="nrl"/>

===Wests Tigers===
[[File:Josh Drinkwater.jpg|thumb|upright|Drinkwater playing for the [[West Tigers]] in 2015]]
On 5 November 2014, he signed a two-year contract with the [[Wests Tigers]], commencing in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/wests-tigers-sign-josh-drinkwater-15599/|title=Wests Tigers sign Josh Drinkwater | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

Drinkwater was initially signed as a back-up for halves [[Mitchell Moses]] and [[Luke Brooks]]. Drinkwater commented on the situation saying, ''"I know I'm behind those boys, [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]] told me straight away that [[Mitchell Moses|"Mitch"]] and [[Luke Brooks|"Brooksy"]] were going to start the year in the first-grade team. I just want to come here, play good footy and if something happens and [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]] calls my name, I've got to take my opportunity".'''''<ref name="nrl" />'''

In round 17 of the [[2015 NRL season]], Drinkwater made his [[West Tigers|Tigers]]' début against the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2015/07/06/late_changes_nrl_rou.html|title=Late Changes: NRL Round 17 vs. Eels – Tigers|publisher=Weststigers.com.au|date=6 July 2015|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 17 May 2016, he was released from his contract at the [[Wests Tigers]] to take up an opportunity with the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/wests-tigers-release-josh-drinkwater-21919/#tIMfGpwHqIfDa0ZK.97|title=Wests Tigers release Josh Drinkwater|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=16 May 2016|accessdate=2016-05-17}}</ref>

Drinkwater subsequently went onto help his new club gain promotion in 2016, back to the top-flight of English [[rugby league]]. Drinkwater left the [[Leigh Centurions]] after a two-year stay at the club, following relegation from the [[Super League]] via the [[Million Pound Game]] in a loss to the [[Catalans Dragons]] at the end of the 2017 season.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Western Suburbs Magpies===
Drinkwater joined [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] side the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] for the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/teams/intrust-super-premiership-nsw/western-suburbs-magpies/josh-drinkwater/ |title=Official Intrust Super Premiership profile of Josh Drinkwater for Western Suburbs Magpies – Wests Tigers |website=weststigers.com.au |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180302103913/https://www.weststigers.com.au/teams/intrust-super-premiership-nsw/western-suburbs-magpies/josh-drinkwater/ |archive-date=2018-03-02}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/josh-drinkwater-set-join-wests-magpies/|title = Josh Drinkwater set to join Wests Magpies|date = 12 February 2018}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
[[File:Josh Drinkwater Catalans.jpg|thumb|upright|Drinkwater playing for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2018]]
On 24 April 2018, Drinkwater signed a one-year deal to join the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/11345250/catalans-dragons-sign-josh-drinkwater-after-luke-walshs-retirement|title = Catalans Dragons sign Josh Drinkwater after Luke Walsh's retirement}}</ref>

On 25 August 2018, Drinkwater was part of the [[Catalans Dragons|Dragons]]' historic [[2018 Challenge Cup]] Final triumph over the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=The Guardian|location=London|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>

Securing the clubs first-ever piece of silverware, Drinkwater who started the game at {{rlp|hb}} went onto kick four goals (8 points), subsequently helping the [[Perpignan]] based side secure a 20–14 victory.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 23 December 2018, it was revealed that he had signed a one-year deal to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/hull-kr-sign-josh-drinkwater-2359868|title=Josh Drinkwater deal is done as Hull KR get their man|date=23 December 2018}}</ref>

On 9 January 2019, Drinkwater received the number 24 jersey ahead of the start of the [[Super League]] season.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

On 13 January 2019, Drinkwater made his non-competitive [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' début in a pre-season friendly against the [[Widnes Vikings]], Drinkwater claimed a 30–16 victory with his new club.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Drinkwater made his first competitive appearance for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in round 1 of the 2019 [[Super League]] season, Drinkwater who added 6 points with the boot recorded a thrilling 18–16 victory over cross-city rivals [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Drinkwater also subsequently picked up the [[Sky Sports]]' 'Man of the Match Award.'{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Drinkwater played his 100th career game on 10 March 2019, in a 6–10 victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]] at [[Belle Vue (Wakefield)|Belle Vue]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

On 4 April 2019, Drinkwater opened his try scoring account for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in a 45-26 [[Super League]] home victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Drinkwater scored a brace of tries on 31 May 2019, in [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' 22-28 [[Challenge Cup]] Quarter Final defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Catalans Dragons (rejoin)===
On 31 October 2019, it was announced that Drinkwater was re-joining [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 October 2019|accessdate=18 January 2021|title=Josh Drinkwater re-joins Catalans|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/drinkwater-re-joins-catalans/}}</ref>

On 9 October 2021, Drinkwater played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
On 23 November 2022, Drinkwater signed a two-year deal to join [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63730072|title=Josh Drinkwater: Warrington Wolves sign Catalans Dragons half-back|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>
Drinkwater played 24 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. Drinkwater played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Drinkwater played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] defeat against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Drinkwater played 23 games for Warrington in the 2024 Super League season as the club reached the semi-final before losing to Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/24654682.warrington-wolves-2024-season-review-february-march/|title=Warrington Wolves 2024 season review, February and March|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 27 November 2024 it was announced by chairman Bill Quinn that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=27 Nov 2024|title=Ex-Warrington Wolves star Josh Drinkwater joins shock UK club|author=Callum Walker|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-warrington-wolves-star-josh-drinkwater-joins-shock-uk-club/}}</ref>
